en: don't go to private detectives.
I'm a consulting detective,
the only one in the world that invented the job. What does that mean?
ko: 이 직업을 발명한 유일한 사람입니다. 그게 무슨 뜻인가요?

en: It means when the police are out of their depth,
which is always, they consult me.
The police don't consult amateurs.
When I met you for the first time yesterday,
I said Afghanistan or Iraq, you looked surprised. Yes, how did you know? I didn't know, I saw.
ko: 아프가니스탄이나 이라크라고 했더니 놀라더군요. 어떻게 알았나요? 몰랐어요, 봤어요.

en: Your haircut, the way you hold yourself says military,
but your conversation as you entered the room. A bit different from my day.
ko: 하지만 방에 들어올 때의 대화는 제 시대와는 좀 다릅니다.

en: Said trained at BART, so army doctor, obvious.
Your face is tanned, but no tan above the wrists.
ko: 얼굴은 햇볕에 그을렸지만, 손목 위는 그렇지 않네요.

en: You've been abroad, but not sunbathing. Your lips really badly warped. You don't ask for a chairman,
ko: 해외에 있었지만, 일광욕은 하지 않았군요. 입술이 많이 변형됐어요. 회장을 요청하지 않아요,

en: you stand like you've forgotten about it.
So it's at least partly psychosomatic.
That says the original circumstances of the injury were traumatic. Wounded in action then.
ko: 그건 부상의 원래 상황이 외상적이었다는 뜻입니다. 전투 중 부상당한 거군요.

en: Wounded in action, Santana, Afghanistan or Iraq. You said I had a therapist.
ko: 전투 중 부상당한, 산타나, 아프가니스탄이나 이라크. 치료사가 있다고 하셨죠.

en: You've got a psychosomatic limp,
of course you've got a therapist. Then there's your brother.
ko: 물론 치료사가 있겠죠. 그리고 당신의 형이 있습니다.

en: Expensive email-enabled MP3 player. Are you looking for a flash?
ko: 비싼 이메일 기능이 있는 MP3 플레이어. 플래시를 찾고 있나요?
